name: Vix Vivax--Romulan Raisin/Walnut Bread
recipeInstructions:
This makes 4 loaves of bread
Scald the milk. Add the butter, sugar, molasses, and salt. Let it cool for about 10 minutes. Then add the sour cream and beat with a wire whisk to mix thoroughly. Pour into a large bowl.
When the liquid is cool enough that a drop on your wrist isn't hot enough to burn, add the yeast (adding yeast to hot mixture will kill the yeast and prevent the bread from rising). Add about 5 cups of flour, 1 cup at a time, mixing well and breaking up lumps. Add raisins and walnuts.
Add flour, 1 cup at a time, until the dough is no longer very sticky; it should be slightly tacky when you've added about 12 cups. Sprinkle flour onto a clean board or countertop. Turn the dough out onto the floured surface and scrape the bowl onto the dough. Wash, dry, and grease the bowl.
Knead the dough for about 15 to 20 minutes using up most of the flour. It should feel stretchy and elastic. Place the dough in the greased bowl and grease the top. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise until doubled in bulk (about 2 hours).
Flour a clean counter, shape the dough into 4 equal parts, and grease 4 bread pans. Shape each portion into a loaf and place in a pan. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour). Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
Beat egg for glaze. Brush tops of loaves before baking at 325 degrees F for 30 minutes, then reduce heat to 300 degrees F and bake another 30 minutes. Loaves should sound hollow when tapped. Remove from oven immediately. Turn out onto cake racks.
The bread is best served warm, but can be cooled and frozen for later use.
